And of course, unconscious competence is a product of deliberate practice and repetition. You've got to go over things over and over and over again. You know, it's just like driving a car, right? The first time you went in the car, you understood where the gear stick was, where the brakes are, where the gas pedal is and the steering wheel. And you understand all the basic fundamentals. And even though you've been taught those fundamentals until you go into the car and then you make a mistake and you learn and you go back in the car and you make another mistake and you learn, you do it over and over and over again. Eventually, guys, what happens? You're an autopilot. You're driving places without even realizing you're driving. Often you're at your destination and you've forgotten how you got there, right? And for that reason, among others, I'm so confident in your success. All right. So we spoke about the offline method at first. If you guys recall, what we did was we went back to the straight line persuasion system and spoke about how things are done over the telephone, right? So there was a straight line and the objective here is to keep control of the sale, keep them on the straight line. And there are boundaries to the top and boundaries at the bottom. If the prospect tries to delete the straight line so far that they actually cross one of the boundaries, we are going to use either rapport or gather intelligence as a means or bringing them back onto the straight line. And once they're on the straight line, we have a few things we need to do or provided we keep them there and we travel them closer and closer to the sale, which of course is our promised land. We are confident that we will accomplish our goals, which leads, of course, to massive wealth and success. Now, we then spoke about the power of the three tens to explain to you guys the value of the three tens. Now, in order to keep them on the straight line and then move them further down it. So we're keeping them on the straight line by gathering intelligence and using rapport, gathering intelligence, using rapport and rapport in the offline world, at least, is a product of your tonality and your body language. OK, in the online world, we do a little bit differently. We use personality in our copy to try to keep them on the straight line, but we're talking offline here. And we know that to keep them on the straight line, we have to accomplish those things. We have to use body language and tonality, which is, of course, our rapport. And we have to gather intelligence, ask really cool, qualifying questions to keep them on that line. Now they're on the line. How do we move them further down it? Right, because we want them to move as far down that line as they can till we reach the sale. That's our objective, the close. And we know that in order to close anybody that's close, but we have to hit the three tens. That's 10 out of 10 for each of these particular factors. One, they've got to love your product. They can't be at a seven. They have to be at a 10, guys. They have to love your product, not like your product, but have a certain amount of confidence in your product. Well, I think your product may be useful. Those kinds of things, between seven and nine, you know, you're talking six to nine, even with those kinds of descriptions, we need to be at a 10. They need to love it. They need to love this product. OK, and then the next 10 we need to accomplish is they must trust and connect with you. They can't think you're OK. They can't think you're a reasonably honest person. They can't think you're a kind of trustworthy salesperson. They need to love and trust you, man. They really need to trust and connect with you. If they don't, you have next to no chance in closing the sale. We need to accomplish a 10, a 10 out of 10. They need to be absolutely connected and have a certain amount of trust, real trust in you as a person. And finally, depending upon your company and your brand and so forth, two and three, you could place one instead of the other, or you may need both, right? Depends on the kind of company you're in. It depends on your industry. It depends on the nature of your business and your sales funnel. But the third and final is they need to feel the exact same way about your company. So they can't trust you and think you're a brilliant salesperson. If you're selling insurance, you can't be the world's greatest insurance salesperson. And you know everything about insurance, but the company that's actually underwriting the insurance is a crap company. And the prospect knows they're a crap company and that prospect doesn't trust that particular company. Has no confidence in it. Has a terrible track record in their friends or people they had dealings with and spoken about before who have used that company and had such a terrible experience. No matter how much they love you, no matter how much they trust and connect with you, they're not buying because you couldn't satisfy the third 10. Right, guys. So we need to get a 10, 10, 10 for those three. And if you can hit those three, 10, 10, 10 at one go, it's just like it's just like a key in the lock. You know, you just you're done. You open up that lock, you'll move all the way to close. And you'll close anybody who is closeable. And our fundamental didn't stop there. Right. We then moved to the offline method of direct marketing. So this was the offline method. We would typically answer an advert. He's saying in a newspaper or a direct mail piece, right? And then they would have a phone number there. And with that phone number, we were expected to call it. And if we called it, we would be given the opportunity to ask for a free product. So there was a free product on offer. If you wanted to claim that free product, then all we have to do is call this particular number. So I'd call that number and then what would happen? They would send over that product and that product was usually like a report or a little book. But what it really was, as opposed to a fully useful and complete content, it was actually a sales letter or a sales message. So the content was useful, but it was incomplete and provided it was incomplete. If the content that was sent to me had a compelling call to action and a compelling offer and they did a great job in selling themselves and their company and their product, then there was a good chance I may actually purchase the full and complete products that was advertised or offered to me. So there's the offline method. Answering a telephone number in the advertisement. I call the number. I get a report sent to me. That report is a sales letter. That sales letter encouraged me to make a purchase and the advertiser makes money. That's how it was done in the past. Then we went on to the online method and we talked about how it was done now. So right now you would actually go onto a website, any particular website and typically you'll be redirected to a squeeze page or a lead page or an opt-in page. They all mean the same thing. And that opt-in page will collect your details and then encourage you to buy something online. So you'll pass on your details, submit your email address and then you'll have an immediate offer, that point of sale that leads you straight to a sales letter. And that sales letter will encourage you to make a purchase. And that's how the advertiser in this particular case will generate revenue. All right. Then we moved on to the secret formula. Now the secret formula is timeless. It's immutable. It's the way it's always been. And it's the way it always will be. You're respective of the media, you're respective of the channel you use, whether it's traffic channel or the funnel channel or how you acquire customers. It doesn't matter. This formula is it. It will never change. It's immutable. So the first thing we need to do is find out who our prospect is. Who exactly are we targeting? Who's our ideal customer? And then we need to find out where they're hanging out. We need to find out where they are. So in this particular example, we said our ideal customer was a fish. Okay. And the fish, where do they hang out? They hang out in the ocean. That's where all the fish are. Right. And then we needed to attract our particular customer or ideal customer with some kind of bait. We needed to attract them onto our line. Right. We need to get them into our sphere of influence, into our world, which you guys later found out was of course our sales funnel. So how can we get these guys into our sales funnel? We need something really attractive. We need them to buy it. So in our particular case, we said we're going to use, you know, some kind of a fish bait, some shrimp or whatever you go or some prawns, whatever it is you're going to put at the end of your line. They're going to bite onto that. And then we need to find out what we're going to do with them. Where are they going to go in our terms? Now that we've gotten as far as we have, we know that it's the sales funnel. At this particular point, all we did was say, we need to do something with that prospect now that we've got them. Are we going to sell them something? Are we going to sell what price? Are we going to sell it to them? Are we going to sell them something else afterwards? So we spoke casually about that particular topic or about that particular segment or step in our secret formula. Now you guys know exactly what it is we do with them and that's put them through a real kick ass sales funnel. All right. Now, then we went on to our basic terminology and you guys were given your basic terminology cheat sheet and your adventure knowledge cheat sheet. So we spoke about the only six lending pages that you guys need to know. So these are all the important ones and you can build just about any sales funnel that you could possibly imagine and believe me, you guys are going to start becoming creative yourselves. You guys have our secret SLM perfect SLM sales funnel. It's not going to be the only one you use. It is going to be the most effective one you use, at least from the outset, at least from the beginning, but you guys are going to start putting your own spin on things and you're going to use your own creativity, which is your only limitation. You're only limited by your creativity with respect to putting together sales funnels. But no matter what sales funnel you build, you're going to be using these pages and these pages are your opt-in page. That's the page you're going to collect email addresses at names, first name, surname, maybe, but definitely email address in almost every case. Then you've got your lead gen page. That's where you're going to collect the exact same thing as your opt-in page name, first name, surname, email address, but it's going to go a little bit further. Because typically lead gen pages are used in online to offline campaigns. So we're going to need probably a phone number, maybe an address, almost always a phone number, frankly, because you're going to have a tele salesperson call up your prospect or customer after they've responded to an online advertisement by opting into a lead generation page. And of course, just as a side note, I hope if you guys are running online to offline campaigns, you're using a lead generation page, I hope very much that your salesperson, whether it's yourself or you have a sales team, following the SLP system and following it down to a T because it is without doubt the single most effective sales system in the world bar none. And that's an absolute guarantee. And I can tell you that from empirical data and evidence, that's just not my opinion, it's a reality, just the single most effective sales system in the world. So I really do hope that if you're running an online offline campaign and the salesperson is touching your leads or your prospects at any point in time, that salesperson is well trained in the SLP sales methodology. Then we spoke about a sales letter. It could be a long form sales letter, for example, which is just a web page that has an enormous amount of persuasive elements on it, an awesome headline. You know, a great call to action. It has a story there. And we spoke about how the sales letter does a lot of the job of the traditional offline salesperson. So the sales letter is expected to build up positives, knockout negatives, pass through the action threshold, ask for the sale. But prior to that, of course, they needed to have established a 310. The sales letter is expected to establish that the prospect likes us or loves us, loves our product, likes and trusts us, likes and trusts our company. All that's the job of our sales letter and our copywriting. And then we spoke about content, article and presale pages and how they can be used to pixel people so we can follow them around the internet. We also spoke about how they can be used to sell one of the 10s. In the case of looping, right? We can loop and we can sell ourselves in a particular banner ad or sell our company maybe in a particular banner ad. And we don't think our sales letter has done a good enough job or a great enough job to actually sell our company up to a 10 in the original sale. All right. So the visitor has since bounced. We could probably send them to a banner and then we can have a content page that actually sells ourselves more aggressively so we can accomplish a 10 there and then push them back into our sales funnel. And we also spoke about how some content pages are particularly useful because some content discovery networks require content pages. They require you send traffic to an article and not straight to an opt-in page or a lead generation page. And we also spoke about how it adds authority, right? You can add a plengi amount of authority to your particular product, to you and or to your company. We spoke about a video sale or video sales letter, which of course is just like a long form sales letter, but in video format, it had a few differences like the checkout pages, times, like if you want to buy a particular product, you have to wait until the video has reached a particular point. And the advertiser is convinced they've passed the action threshold because if they haven't passed the action threshold, they're not going to show you the buy button because you're not going to buy. You're going to click it to check the price. You haven't been sufficiently sold yet. You haven't been sold with respect to the actual advertiser himself, his product and his or her company, of course. So what happens is they time that buy button. So you watch an entire video and you have to wait a certain period of time until you can actually buy it. And those of you who have a click funnels users or lead page users, you can absolutely set up a VSL with a timed buy button in the software, in the editor. And it's so, so easy. You just pick a template with a VSL, you click that buy button and then you look at the triggers and then you just list how long you want until the actual buy button appears, how many seconds, how many minutes, whatever it is you want to do. Then we talk about webinar sign up pages because webinars are great sales funnels, especially for existing customers and clients. So if you actually have a list of clients and you want to send them to a webinar where you'll then make an offer and sell them a really cool product that they're really going to love and they're going to be able to make a lot of use for a webinar, maybe a great idea and it can form a really good sales funnel. Then we spoke about some basic terminology we carried on with our terminology about affiliate marketing, affiliate and CPA networks. Of course, affiliate marketing would involve you selling other people's products and networks are where all those products sit. We spoke about how affiliate marketing is a great market research tool, but I didn't necessarily recommend you guys become full time affiliate marketers. Rather, I would much rather you guys actually have your own products and then have an army of affiliates selling your products. And that would mean you can put your products on the networks. Alternatively, you can use software like ClickFunnels to create your own affiliate army. Each of them will be given a link. You'll give them a link and you say send traffic to that link every time somebody makes a purchase. I'm going to give you a certain percentage and you just have your army go out to work for you. That's the way I wanted you guys to do it. And I hope you take my advice and that respect. We spoke about leads. So leads are just somebody who's made it into your sales funnel, but haven't purchased anything yet, but we can use that obviously to market to. We can further drive them down our sales funnel at point of sale right away, or we can use email marketing or retargeting a whole bunch of other cool things to actually get that lead back into our sales funnel so that we can convert that lead into a customer. We spoke about email lists. You guys all know what an email list is after say opt in, they become members of our email list. We spoke about conversions. So take somebody taking the next step. We mainly spoke about somebody converting from one page to another, but we also know they're in a micro conversions as well. Not nearly as important as these macro conversions, like moving from an opt in page into a self liquidating offer page into a core offering page and so forth. I shared that example where we had a client who was a an education institution and every time somebody clicked the live chat button, they would make money. I mean, the course advisor at the other end who was speaking to the prospective students on live chat was doing a great job at converting everybody. So as long as we could convince a particular visitor to click that live chat button, we knew our client was going to make a lot of money. So what we do is set up a micro conversion and Google analytics and we tried our best to redirect visitors as best we could with a whole bunch of arrows and some cool copy and we changed the elements and put the live chat in different places and tested and tested and tested until we found out the perfect formula or as close to perfect as we could that would allow for the most amount of visitors who actually land on that particular page of this website to engage the course advisor through live chat. We spoke about upsells and downsoles, which was our introduction to sales finals. We spoke about impressions. Impression is just when the ad appears. Right? So we spoke about how impressions aren't necessarily the same as served ads. We just needed to be mindful that not every ad that was actually served was seen because we know that some ads are above the fold and some ads are below the fold and even though the ad has been loaded on the actual web page, if it was below the fold, it wouldn't have even been seen. Right? It needs to be above the fold. Of course, above the fold is anything that you can see without touching your cursor and without scrolling down. Then we spoke about traffic sources. We spoke about a lot of traffic sources and then we spoke about click through rates, which of course is the amount of people who have seen your ad compared against the amount of people who have clicked that ad. That is expressed as a percentage in the click through rate. We spoke about raw clicks and unique clicks. Of course, raw clicks are the amount of people who have clicked your ad or your link or whatever, no matter what. So if it's the same person who's clicking it over and over and over again, that's a raw click. That is a unique click. If it's only one IP address, that's clicked at once in 24 hours. So if I had a particular ad, say my competitors ad and I clicked that a hundred times, my competitor looks and says, wow, I must have a really great ad because it's got a click through rate of 100 and it's only been up for a minute or it's only been up for a couple of minutes. But that's not a true reflection of the success of the ad or it's not a true reflection of how attractive that particular ad is because the click through rate has been greatly distorted and skewed because they're looking at raw clicks. What's important, of course, is unique clicks, the unique clicks. That 100 that I clicked in that first minute would only be one unique click as opposed to the 100 raw clicks. Then we spoke about direct links and affiliate links and there are of course the links that affiliates are given by the vendors. We spoke about tracking links. If you want to know which traffic source performed better than the next, you're typically going to want to have a tracking link and that link is just a unique link that you would have generated and you would have sent traffic to this unique link. So not the actual link of your funnel page, but a unique special link that's just yours and you would have lots of them and you can title this link, for example, I'm going to call this link a rented email list. I'm going to call the other link PPV traffic and then you're going to find out where most of your traffic is coming from. Is it most of it coming from rented email list? Is most of it coming from PPV traffic and so forth. We spoke about split tests and split tests are just we spoke about it and we actually went through it very quickly when we went to click fun ones. We just talked about how we can put different pages up online, right? It doesn't need to be the same page. We can create a page and have a rotator link and click funnels does this in the background. You don't have to worry about it, but if you don't have click funnels, you may just want to purchase a rotator link, which is just a piece of software where you load up a whole bunch of different URLs and then they'll spit out this one URL and this one URL is all those URLs on rotation. So what would happen is if you want to test your landing page, for example, you can put up five different landing pages with a different element on each. Maybe you change the headline on each or something like that and they're going to rotate around the net. So you've got five. So somebody clicks your URL. They're going to see it once they click the URL again. They're going to see a different one and so forth and so forth. And that way when you get your results, you can look at which of the landing pages was the most effective. Otherwise, you can make it even easier for yourself and then just use software that already has built in split tests like click funnels where you can just create your own page, whatever page you want in your funnel and then you have a option to just create a test and you can just say plus, which is a plus test, create test plus and then they'll clone that existing page. You make whatever changes you want and then click funnels will tell you which particular page won the test. And then of course, we spoke about frequency caps. How many times you're prepared to have somebody see your ad? Okay, because you don't want the same person seeing your ad over and over and over again if they've proven themselves to be unqualified and they shouldn't be in our funnel. So they're out. Why are we spending money trying to reach these guys? They've said no and they've said no enough time. So we said, okay, three to five times at the very most, we're prepared to have somebody see our ad depending on the channel. Then we moved on to copywriting, right? Guys, we wanted to work out how to assemble a kick ass sales letter. So we've only shared our secret formula to writing irresistible sales letters, but we will always mindful of the reality. And that's that the straight line persuasion system. S or P is the backbone. It's the foundation for everything we do, especially our copywriting, especially our copywriting. Okay, so our copywriting largely is a product of straight line persuasion. We spoke about how we can convert scripts into sales letters. We spoke about how some of the greatest copywriters in the world have come back and said, well, the ideal sales letter, the sales letter that's going to convert the highest is really just a sales conversation for a sale script, put it into written words and uploaded online. Now that's what's going to convert the highest. That's what the top guys have said. You know, guys like Gary Halbert, the late and great Gary Halbert, that said that Jordan Belfort came up with this assessment without having heard any of this from any of the copywriting experts. He said the exact same thing to me. And that's when it really resonated with me after I'm hearing the same thing from people who don't know each other, both great guys in their industries, you know, just tight ends of industry. They're all saying the same thing. So I knew it had to be the case and I put it to the test. Now we're absolutely right. The highest converting sales letter you can ever put online is a sales letter that was at one point a sales script. So Jordan gave you a whole bunch of sales scripts and we gave you a whole bunch of sales letters. I wanted you guys to take a look at traditional copywriting letters, retrace them, write them down over and over again so you can get a feel of why they converted so well, why they've done so well, and of course do the same with the sales scripts. When we actually analyzed the sales letters, we analyzed the sales letter process. We reminded you all guys of the three 10s, told you how important the three 10s are. Your sales copy must establish the three 10s. It has to. And the thing is, even people who didn't know the three 10s, master copywriters who had no idea of what the three 10s were, they were never trained in the straight line persuasion system. They just they had it all. They knew it intuitively. They don't even know why their sales letters work, but guys like us who actually take a scientific approach and analytical approach, guys like all of us here in the straight line marketing community, we knew the value of the three 10s and we can actually look at a highly converting very successful piece of copy and we can say, Oh, well, there it is. No wonder it converted so well. You've done a great job in selling the product and then you made sure they trusted and connected with you and they thought you're an authority and you're a billion person. And then of course, you didn't forget to mention how great your company was and the awesome track record your company may have. Right. So and then we introduced the concept of the action threshold, which of course is one of the foundational concepts of the straight line persuasion system. But we applied it online, right? So we spoke about how the action threshold needs to be established and passed in order for you to ask for the sale, which is why a lot of long form sales pieces only have the sale price or the option to buy the click button, the click key to purchase the add to card button after at the end of it, after there's so much sale has been done, so much selling, excuse me, has been done, so much copy has been written. It's also the reason that they have those timed buttons on VSLs where you can't actually purchase the product until a certain period of time because the advertiser needs to have passed the action threshold and these guys don't even know why it works. They just ran so many tests and they've decided that, okay, well, we have to put the buy button at the end. We can't put it at the front of this letter. It's going to kill out. It's killing our conversions and they're looking at the numbers, but they're not looking at the psychology. And the same thing happened with the VSL. People know intuitively that it makes more sense for the buy button to be hidden until a certain point in time. And that point in time, we know a straight line marketers is when the visitor or the viewer has themselves passed the action threshold, which is of course, is an unconscious set of points that somebody has to be motivated in order to take action. All right, so here's the process when we're writing copy involved in any kind of sale. The process is to tip the scale, right? So you need to keep on stacking up those positives. You need to talk more about your product. You need to talk more about your company. You need to talk more about yourself. You need to add up all those positives, right? You need to start stripping the prospects of any kind of negatives, stack positive, strip negatives. And as soon as the scale balances in favor of the positives, then you're going to typically pass through the action threshold. That's the objective. I also asked you guys to vision the action threshold as an invisible line, an invisible line that only once crossed, have you earned the right to ask for the sale? And that doesn't change a thing. Now that we're online, as opposed to offline, there's still an invisible line. There's an invisible line in your copy and your long form sales letter. There's an invisible line in your VSLs. There is an invisible line that needs to be passed. And that invisible line is when the positives outweigh the negatives. And at that point in time, you guys can ask for the sale. And you can see why it's largely an art, putting this stuff together. It's an art, but one that can be guided by a certain amount of science, right? It's an art. You need to be creative and you need to use your instincts and intuition to come up with your assessment as to whether or not the action threshold has indeed been passed. We spoke about the formula for writing copy. The step one was features and step two was benefits. And step three was story. Of course, the story was expected to suck in the prospect. That was its objective and features were what was in your product, you know, the little bits and pieces, the components. Step two, which will benefit was, of course, what those bits and pieces, what those components meant to your prospect, what it did for your prospect. Remember the sole test and what that means to you test. That's how we turn a feature into a benefit. Of course, again, the story sucks in the prospect. It uses emotion as a vehicle to drive the prospect down our sales page. Then we had a summary, which we were supposed to stack all the benefits and all the features and put these on top of that, on top of that to really express to our audience just the real value of what it is they have or the real opportunity they have in front of them in taking advantage of our offer. And then we came to the headline as opposed to starting with the headline. And we spoke about how a headline needs to do what a salesperson over on a phone call has to accomplish in the offline world. They need to grab attention. You've only got a couple of seconds. So it doesn't change whether it's online or offline. You just that headline, that first thing they see needs to really grab the prospect's attention. And then finally, we spoke about a call to action because no matter how brilliant your sales letter is, if you're not actually asking somebody to do something, then nothing is going to be done. No matter how great it is, no matter how many people have seen it, no matter how much traffic you send to it, ultimately, you need to ask somebody to do something and not just expect that because you spoke about how great your product is, they're going to buy your product. You need to absolutely sell your product, absolutely sell yourself and your company. And then you need to ask for the order, right? Jordan always teaches it. Jordan's having consulted with probably more sales teams than anybody else in the world has come back to me and told me, you know, the number one reason sales don't close because people don't ask for the order. Salespeople are too scared to us for the order or they're frankly too ignorant to us for the order. And what you need to do is be smarter and braver than that. You need to eventually ask for the order after you pass the action threshold. Not only only earn the right to us for the order, but it is frankly your duty to ask for the order. You need to do that because you've sold a great product, you've created a great product, you have a great deliverable. You're only doing your customers and clients good by actually encouraging them or empowering them to make the decision or take the step into doing something that you know is for their benefit. Then we spoke about what was the theme of the course from this point onwards. Those of you who remember what it is, I'm sure you do. I hope you guys are yelling it out on the other end. There's only one rule that matters in business and this carried us through the rest of the course. And what's that rule? We're just able and willing to spend the most amount of money to acquire a customer wins. We spoke about this in enormous detail because it's so important. We know that if a customer is worth a certain amount of money to us and that certain amount is more than he or she is worth to our competitors, then we can spend more to acquire that particular customer than our competitors can spend, which means we can grow much faster and we can capture as much market share as we want. And that was an immutable business law and it's something highly countercultural, counterintuitive. It's not the way most people think and I'm glad most people don't think that way. I'm glad as guys like us in the straight line marketing community who think that way because it's really thin the herd. There aren't too many people that are going to think this way, which means that we really do have an unfair advantage and an edge over the rest of the market. Okay, then we spoke about how we were going to increase the customer value because we know if the customer's worth more to us, then we can spend more money to acquire them and we said that the best way to do it is by creating an awesome sales funnel. But we also noted that it's kind of a misnomer, right? Because you can increase conversions and that increasing conversion of your sales funnel, of course, would mean that they could spend more to acquire more customers because the funnel is itself converting more people so they're getting more customers for from their ad spend. But we then made mention of the 80-20 principle and we spoke about what's the one thing? What's the one thing that matters more than anything else in increasing the value of my customer and allowing me to spend more than anybody else? And we came to the conclusion that that one thing, that big thing, that 20% that was going to lead to 80% of the results is of course sales funnels. And then we introduced you guys to sales funnels which became the guiding theme of the rest of this course. So our sixth step to the perfect straight line marketing sales funnel was up next. We had an opt-in bribe which the objective of an opt-in bribe was just to get a lead. That's it. All we wanted is a lead. We don't want to make any money yet. We just wanted a lead. That's it. And then we used that lead and made them an offer. And the purpose of the offer which is our self-liquidating offer or our SLO was to make our money back from the traffic costs or from any of the costs associated with acquiring that particular customer. So that was the objective. We haven't planned to make any money yet. We've put an opt-in bribe. We purchased some traffic. We drove them to an opt-in page. They've submitted their email. Then they've purchased something small and we still haven't made anything at best. We've broken even. We're okay with that because we're smart marketers. And what we do next, we offer them a core offering, a core product. And that was where we're going to make our money. That's our margin maker. That's what we planned on selling them right from the outset. Only we're more likely to sell them our core product than our competitors are because we've sold them a very cheap product. And by cheap, I don't mean low quality. I mean low in price, high in quality and value because they just got it. The customer, think of the customer psychology, he's just made a purchase or taken advantage of a purchase that he knows was a great value and he got it so cheap. So he loves you. So just the sales funnel in and of itself does the job of having the customer like and trust you, like and trust your company. So the sales funnel is actually working as a straight line persuader. It's amazing how it all functions and all comes together. So once they've actually made that purchase, then the objective here is to break even and of course, convert a prospect into a customer because once they part their money, they become a customer and every study known to man in this space as concluded that customer is more likely to become a repeat customer and buy something else from you 10 times more likely in fact, at least to buy something else from you than somebody who has never purchased anything from you again. And then they're going to buy that core product. We're going to make our margins and then depending on our market, we're going to create a series of upsells and downsells to further increase the value of our customers who allows us to spend even more to acquire him or her. And then we also have our funnel follow up system, which functions to bring people back into the funnel where they dropped off. So if somebody has opted in, but haven't purchased our self liquidating offer, we're going to use our funnel follow up system, which was a very sophisticated system that went through and we spoke about all the different ways you can follow people up. And the objective, of course, of that follow up was to get them back into that step of the sales funnel that they had since dropped off at. Here it was. We love this funnel. We just gave it to you guys. Here it is. Take it. It's yours. And we taught you guys how to build it in click funnels, right? So you're going to have a lead page and then it's going to run into a sales page, which is going to be as cheaper offer. And that's going to lead into an order form. That order form is going to submit. And once it submits, you're going to have an immediate one click upsell. And that's going to be your core offering. And then you're going to either purchase or decline. If you decline, you have a series of downsells or you have a downsell. If you purchase, you'll get an upsell. If you decline that upsell, you'll get a downsell and so on and so forth. And you just need to use your creativity in that back end to increase the value of your customer in as many ways as you like. Ultimately, of course, culminating in a thank you page. Then we spoke about paid traffic, right? And we purposely put paid traffic after the funnel page because we know now that traffic is a commodity. It's been commoditized, guys. It isn't what it used to be. There aren't any special little traffic tricks and hacks that are going to cause an avalanche of free traffic to land on your landing page without you having to do anything. Those days are over. And if anybody tells you otherwise, you know, hold on to your wallet. It's just not a reality. But paid traffic, although it's a commodity, we still need to know two things, right? We need to know the best place for us to go. And we need to know how to effectively and efficiently target the traffic. So it spoke a lot about networks, what networks we should use. And then we of course spoke about how we should effectively and efficiently target our traffic. And here we go. The goal of when we purchase traffic is to profit or at least break even at that point of sale, right? So you want to send traffic to a particular page, say an opt-in page, then you want them to maybe at the point of sale, get an invitation for a self-liquidating offer. And then at that point of sale, after having completed or converted from the self-liquidating offer, you've broken even now. So even if they don't purchase your core product or your core offering at point of sale and they drop off, we can still use our funnel follow-up system to bring them back into the funnel. And then of course, to market to them through email and so on and so forth. We've used that lead page to capture the email address and the information. So now we know exactly how to reach and target that particular customer. And of course, we've also used retargeting and a whole bunch of other things because we put pixels on the page, which of course are those badges that allow us to follow up on customers who have since dropped off on our funnel. So what do we want to do? We want to buy traffic and we want it to want to profit from every single campaign we run at the very least we want to break even. So we want them to at least pie that self-liquidating offer so we can break even. And then even if they don't purchase further, we still have their email address and we have their details and we plan on marketing to them in the future. We spoke about some of our favorite traffic platforms. YouTube, LinkedIn, Google, Twitter, internet, email is Facebook, Spanner ads, PPV, CPA, content, discovery platforms and the list goes on and on and on and on. You guys are spoiled for traffic channels. They're everywhere. That's why I'm trying to impress upon you guys that traffic is just a commodity. You've just go to any of these traffic stores and give me your credit card and it's as simple as that. It doesn't get easier. You just need to know how to target the traffic. Then we spoke about Dan Kennedy's marketing triangle. Right? We spoke about how you need to have a particular market and you need to have a perfect message that matches that particular market and then you need to use the most appropriate media when submitting that message to that market. Right? And when we spoke about traffic, we were speaking about that media. So we needed the most ideal traffic platform for our market, for them to be responsive for our message to resonate with them. So we spoke about the value of the Dan Kennedy marketing triangle. And then it was time for us to gather intelligence. We spoke about the customer avatar. You guys will recall that we created a fictional character and that was our ideal customer. And the reason that was so important to us is because when we're targeting traffic, it's all a product of who it is we're trying to speak to. We need them to be wherever this traffic channel is. Right? So if we're targeting to, we're targeting traffic to a 70, 80 year old prospect, then why will on earth re-advertise on Facebook? Why would we advertise on Twitter? Even worse because Twitter has a much younger demographics or a much younger user base than Facebook. So you need to understand your customer. And the best way to do that is by gathering market intelligence and creating for yourself your own customer avatar, which is a fictional character of your ideal customer. You just no need to stop at one. You can create a series of customer avatars and keep them in mind because not only are they helpful in targeting traffic, they are super helpful when we're writing our copy because that way we can look at our customer avatar and we can say, I'm writing to you. I want to sell you on myself, on my product, on my company. And it makes it so much easier rather than trying to have a conversation to so many people at the same time. It becomes a lot more personal, effective and converts at a much higher rate. If you structure your copy in a way where you're actually having a conversation with one person, that one person, of course, would be your ideal customer, which will be your customer avatar. All right. So then we spoke about their email marketing mastery. So we spoke about our special email formula. We spoke about deliverability issues. We talked about email list hygiene. We spoke about technical setups. We spoke a lot about emails, right? We spoke about our formula and let's just move straight on to our formula because that was the most important thing that we spoke about with email and we spoke about how we've got our opt-in right. We've got that particular email address now because they've come into our opt-in page, which is the first page of our sales funnel. Now from there, no matter what point they drop off at, we still need to indoctrinate them in that we need to introduce them to us. We need to introduce them to the way we do business. We need to introduce them to us, to our company, to our product. This is three times time. Right. And we gave you our indoctrination template. This is the template we use as soon as somebody enters our sphere of influence, which is to say whenever they become a prospect. So they've actually submitted their email address and once they become a lead, we have to send them our indoctrination email. And at the same time, we then send them our straight line content email number one. And that has a lot of straight line persuasive elements in it and has followed up with a one-two punch with the straight line content email number two, either the next day or the day following that. And then that would, of course, encourage and lead to sales. And we gave you the template. So for you guys, all that you had to do was fill in the blanks, nice and simple, upload them into the email order responder solution that you chose. We had a walkthrough through GetResponse. It was up to you which software or platform you decided to use and simply set up the calendar in the order responder section, press go, and you're off. We also spoke about how this integrates with ClickVunals. So we spoke about ClickVunals in the funnel section and the funnel module. Then we came back to it in the email module because we needed to integrate our web page builder with our email marketing software, our order responder solution, so that once somebody opts in to one of our pages that have been built in ClickVunals, the email marketing engine will go to work and start sending these emails, the indoctrination email, the straight line email, the straight line email content too. And of course, they're ultimately leading to sales pages, which of course is our 24 hour salesman. All right, so you're making money in your sleep with email marketing and it's the only channel that you can actually say that with complete confidence. The only way to really make money while in your sleep is once you have an awesome email engine working for you 24 seven 24 hours a day, seven days a week is doing all the heavy lifting, it's applying all the straight line persuasive elements. And it's a great example of straight line marketing that play. Then we spoke about looping and we spoke about how important the fundamentals here were with looping and Jordan came in and he spoke about looping with respect to how he applies it in the offline world and that of course is the genesis. That's the foundation of looping online and looping is a way for us to circumnavigate or to dodge objections, deflect objections and then resell ourselves, our product and our company because we need those three tends to be aligned perfectly in order for us to get a sale. And if the prospect has left our page, so left any particular page on our sales funnel and haven't made it to the next page, then we know one of those three 10s are off. So what do we do? We use retargeting technology to do just that. So either ourselves, our product or our company. And we showed you examples of how we can do each. And there again is our sales funnel and we have at the bottom the funnel follow up. And it's this particular funnel follow up that is of course what we're talking about when we explore the strategy and the technique of online looping. So if somebody's on our sales page, but they haven't converted to our upsell and we're going to use our tracking pixels to follow that visitor or retarget that visitor and we resell either ourselves, our products or our company. And we're going to do so by creating banners, great banners that accomplish just that. And then they may send to depending on how confident we are that we've actually solved that particular 10 that we're trying to resell in the banner, but depending on how well we've sold it, we may either send them straight to the sales page that they had since dropped off at or alternatively, we could send them to a pre sell page that does an even more aggressive job in selling that 10 and also coupling it with the other two 10s and then directing them to a sales page or the sales page that they had dropped off at and is totally up to us and it depends upon how well we think we have sold those 10s to that particular prospect in that particular case. Then we spoke about how to actually get this online, get this machine all going, get it all moving. And we spoke about the different kind of networks you could use, whether it was at roll or retarget or Facebook, Facebook custom audiences or Google ad words or perfect audience, which we actually had a look inside of. We had a walkthrough for perfect audience and we had a look at how we could actually get the banners that we created, either we built them ourselves in banner stack, if you guys remember, or we went on to five or up working, got somebody to build them for us and it was a matter of just uploading those banners, creating the lists or creating the campaigns and then, of course, putting the pixels, the pixels that perfect audience would have generated for us, putting them onto our page and then setting up the system with respect to how you actually want to target people, whether it's based on what page they've been on or what the paid URL is or so on and so forth, just some basic little targeting things that we had to take care of and perfect audience. And then we just submitted and it went live and went to work for us. All right. And then we spoke about our secrets to almost always crushing the competition. This was the spooky module guys. This is the module that introduced you guys to our secrets to competitive intelligence and market analysis. So what did we do? We introduced you guys to the four steps to online domination. This is our four steps to market and competitor research and analysis. The step one was you needed to find out who you're leading competitors were. We done that through a variety of ways. We started off with Google. We looked at the organic ads. We looked at the paid ads. And then we went to SEM Russian. We looked at the competitor section. We found out more about our competitors and you'll notice as we moved throughout the rest of the steps, we kept on finding out more commercial terms that people were typing in to actually find our competitors. So we're going back, taking those terms, throwing them back into Google, finding more competitors, going into SEM Rush, finding more competitors. And our competitive list kept on growing, growing and growing. So once we found our competitors, we wanted to identify our highest value prospects and customers. We of course done that by booking at our own list. If we had a list of buyers, what we would do is actually data mine that list. We could do that through a service called Tower Data. And we would actually go upload that list and find out as much we can about our customers. We'd also look at what our competitors were doing, all the kind of customers that would visit our competitors sites by putting in our competitors URLs into websites or solutions like Quantcast and like Alexa. And we tried to gather as much information about our prospects as possible. Facebook also has a tool where you can do this, that we didn't explore. So you guys can do some research on that to find even more about your prospects and customers. You want to find as much psychographic and demographic information as possible, which of course is going to help us with our copywriting, because we know more about who we're going to write. We can actually amend our avatar at this point because we know even more about him or her and it's absolutely going to help us with targeting. So we're going to be able to target our traffic really effectively and efficiently because we have all this information about our prospect. And then we use data to build the perfect funnel. We know exactly who our prospect is. Right? We know exactly who our competitors are. Now it's just a matter of getting that competitor, throwing in their URL into the Google Keyword Planner, find out what people are looking for when Google suggests particular ad groups. And we know that people are actually looking actively for these particular things, right? And we use those things to build our funnel. And we also didn't stop there, but we also used other competitive tools to further dig into what it is our competitors want. We used merchant words, which spoke directly to Amazon and it gave us the search volume for particular terms in Amazon. And we were able to type that in to find out what our customers are looking for. And we've done a very similar thing with an eBay related tour, which was of course, Terapeak, which is a piece of software that communicates directly with eBay and has a real eBay data. And they're able to tell us what people are earning. So what are what sellers are earning selling a particular thing? So we know exactly what our customers want. We know the demand and we're able to build our funnel with that in mind. We then also looked at the Amazon bestseller list, not only to generate ideas for things to include into our sales funnel, but also give us ideas on the kind of copywriting that works really well, because we know that most books are solved off the back of the power of the actual title of the book, right? Which can function as a headline for our sales copy. So we will take a look at what people in our market really resonated with by looking at those top titles, maybe putting them in the list and we will be creating kind of like a swipe file with all these core titles. And they can we can convert them into headlines, email subject lines. We can do all this fun and cool stuff with it. And we know it's a series of words that our customers really do resonate with. And then we wanted to identify the best traffic source and ads. So we went to a whole bunch of different tools that went to similar web. We looked at similar web in a lot of details, right? We spoke about how we can get our ads and that's going to tell us our traffic source. Of course, we spoke about how we can actually find out the ads through other software, right? So we go into similar web and find out that a particular competitor is getting a lot of search traffic and most of the search traffic or a lot of the search traffic isn't organic, but rather it's actually paid. So in practice, what it means is that particular customer is paying for a lot of Google AdWords search, particularly not the GDN, not the display. So we would actually go into our keywords by tool. We type in that customer and we take a look at their ads because we know that their ads are working because they're spending a lot of money on those ads. And that's where most of their traffic is coming from. So we went into Keyword Planner. We filtered the results based on ROI. So Keyword Planner has a sophisticated enough algorithm to give us an idea of which of the ads are making the most amount of money for our clients. And we picked the copy of the best ads and we would model our ads around them because our customers have done a lot of tests, our competitors have done a lot of testing. They've gone to the market, they found out what worked and what didn't. And we would definitely model those winners, just the right thing to do. And then we also spoke about different platforms. We could do the exact same thing, but you could do it with display ads. And we explored what runs where, which can accomplish that and gives us an idea of exactly what banner ads are working for our clients, what banner ads they're using. And we also had a quick discussion about AdBeat, which accomplished the exact same thing. And then we spoke about the versatility of the straight line marketing system. We spoke about the straight line marketing system as a real system, a set of tools and skills that can be used in so many different ways. It's not limited to online marketing, right? You can use all of the skills that you guys have acquired throughout this course in offline campaigns, offline to online campaigns, then online to offline campaigns. You're only limited by your creativity. That's why I love this system. It's so universal. It's so versatile. It's a powerful, so effective. And you guys are all starting to see that now. You've made it so far. You can really see the versatility of it. I don't doubt. And we spent a particular amount of effort and attention on the offline to online because that was where we could exercise most amount of creativity. We had more options in offline to online as opposed to online to offline. If you recall, online to offline, most of what we spoke about was the telemarketing team doing a great job selling people after they've actually had that lead generated by online efforts. That was the bulk of our online to offline efforts. But then we spoke about all the options we had offline to online. So we spoke about offline lead gen mechanism. All right, so that's just an offline traffic source. And that traffic source would send particular visitors to our regular online, straight line marketing sales funnel. That's it. The objective of the offline channel was to send traffic to our online sales funnel. And we spoke about the different offline lead gen mechanisms that we had at our disposal. So we had what we had a newspaper advertisement. We spoke about two different kinds of newspaper ads that we could make use of. We spoke about the standard ad space with the little classified ads. And then we spoke about the advertorials, which, of course, were the native ads or the ads that followed the form and function of whatever publication that they were in. OK, so we spoke about when we were to extend the space ad, we spoke about options and networks that you could go to. We followed a couple of websites or one website. We went through nationwide advertising. We spoke about how we could actually purchase ad space from nationwide advertising and a few tips and tricks. And we spoke about how you should start small. And once your ads actually generating a lot of sales, you can actually expand very rapidly and quickly by purchasing the larger packages that circulate around seven million, that have like a seven million weekly circulation, 10, 20 million weekly circulation, nationwide circulation. We spoke about how we can get into so many newspapers so quickly, but we also were mindful of the fact that we needed to have a great headline, a great body, a great CTA and especially a nice and simple URL for our readers to be able to remember and to input into either their phones or their desktops. And they have to actually directly input this URL into their search box. So it needed to be really easy. And we also stress the importance of making a great offer, you know, offering them something for free, offering them something is significantly lower price than they would ordinarily expect to pay for it. And then we spoke about advertorials, those native ads. And we spoke with native ads, we have to approach it a little bit differently. We decided it made more sense to go to a media buyer. So we spoke about MarkerMax and we spoke about how it was a great tool for you guys to actually find a media buyer and have a conversation with him or her. And we spoke about before actually doing that, you needed to get your advertorial design. We spoke about going to Fiverr to get a design and you can get a design for a few bucks. We have to just give them your content, giving your images and they can turn it into a great looking newspaper, right? Or at least a newspaper design piece of creative. And that was now our only option. We also spoke about going on to Upwork and Upwork.com. It will be a little bit more expensive, but we can actually list our project details and invite people to read and see and have a look through all these great proposals and all these great portfolios until we found somebody who we were convinced could create an awesome advertorial for us. And all that was left for us is of course to go to our media buyer, give them our creative, give them our targeting instructions and tell them to test small and if it works, let's scale quickly. Then we spoke about radio and TV. We spoke about our broadcast strategy, very little known particular strategy that was actually an opportunity for you guys to purchase airspace so that you could actually host your own radio show. So we spoke about the benefits of doing that, which would include a whole bunch of authority inviting some of your Dream 100 lists to actually come in as interviewees and you could interview them yourself and develop that relationship. It's a great way to circumnavigate and navigate around the gatekeeper. So we spoke about all the benefits of a broadcast strategy and how you can use it, especially in the B2B space for really great wins. And then we spoke about traditional direct marketing approach, which of course is just a normal television or radio ad with a compelling call to action that directs people to a nice and easy to remember URL, which of course is our URL for our opt-in page, the first page on our sales funnel. Now, then we spoke about online to offline campaigns. But prior to speaking about offline to online campaigns, there was actually one element that wasn't listed here and that was our direct mail pieces. If you guys recall, the direct mail pieces were another offline to online strategy, but we would actually send people direct mail and with the objective of actually getting them online. And we said that a great B2B direct mail approach would be the 3D mail where we had little attention grabbers like a garbage bin or a bank bag or a bottle of pills. And then we'd have a headline that connected the actual grabber to our particular product in some way so that it was a little more congruent. So the our prospect would understand why it is we've actually included a little garbage bin or garbage can or rubbish bin into this particular newsletter, into our envelope and then sent it off to them. So we'd have a sales letter with a great little clever headline. It's cute. I said it can be cheesy and it absolutely can be, but it absolutely also works and it served a few benefits. Firstly, we knew it had to be at the top of the pile because it was oddly shaped. If you have an envelope with an actual physical object inside, you can't put it at the bottom of the stack of envelopes because all the envelopes would fall. So you had to put it right up the top. And then after that, outside of that, another benefit was that it typically was able to get straight to our decision maker and bypass the receptionist or all the gatekeeper because the gatekeeper will typically think that this was an important piece of mail. And it also be sent directly to the decision maker. It doesn't appear to be a sales piece. So we spoke about it to value, but we also recognize it was expensive. It wasn't cheap. At least not when compared to running simple, cheap little direct campaigns, direct mail campaigns like postcards. So we said that you need to have a really high customer value for this campaign to work, which is why we thought you made most sense. And I hope you guys all agree that it makes far more sense for you to run a 3D mail campaign if you are a B2B business, but not a B2C business. But for B2C, they do have their own little tricks. And one of the great strategies for direct mail for B2C, of course, is the postcard and the postcard needs to have a compelling offer on it. And that offer or to really incentivize the prospects to go straight to their computers or straight to their phones, type in the URL of your first page of your opt-in page to take advantage of whatever it is you're offering them on that postcard. And then we'll let the funnel do all the heavy lifting, indirecting people from prospects to customer to higher value customer to even higher value yet and so on and so forth. And then we spoke about online or offline and online or offline was a unique way of actually setting people through a sales funnel because typically it is done through the telephone. So you would actually have a lead generation page and that lead generation page. The primary objective there is to get a telephone number. You could also include an address, but the primary objective is to get a telephone number. And with that telephone number, a trained salesperson, an SLP trained salesperson is expected to pick up the call and then convert the prospect, especially to higher ticket sales, because we know that it's typically very challenging to sell anything over $5,000 online. So you would almost always need to speak to somebody real, a real person, whether it's over the phone or face to face, but there kind of needs to be an offline communication with a real person in order to sell those higher ticket products. So we said that the most effective way to use online to offline was to create a great online campaign that generates as many leads as possible. And those leads are given to trained salespeople and those salespeople by the sell to those leads in person or over the phone. And we gave the example of, of course, a real estate agent who could run a offline online campaign that invites prospects or visitors to submit a lead generation form in exchange for an accurate estimation of the value of their home based on local prices and so on and so forth. And they to take advantage of that, the homeowner would then submit their details along with their phone number and their address. And then the real estate agent or the real estate broker would then go ahead and organize a time to visit that person and make a sale face to face where they would attempt to get the listing.
